name: Training & Preparation
subtitle: Build the routine that wins before the match starts
description: Aim routines, decision review, studying demos as an individual and as a team. Learn what to practice, how often, and what to look for when watching your own demos or players with your role.

name: Mechanical Skills
subtitle: Crosshair, movement, utility — the raw fundamentals
description: Crosshair placement, sensitivity, spray control, shoulder peeks, wide peeks and utility lineups. The mechanics you need to execute your read.

name: Game Knowledge
subtitle: META, strategies, tricks and money management
description: How to read META shifts, adjust strategies mid-match, pick smart tricks (positions, utilities) and manage your own, your team's, and the opponent's economy.

name: Communication
subtitle: Tone, information density, calls that don't waste
description: What to cover, what you see, what you do — and how tone changes the round. Dense comms, clean callouts, and the discipline to shut up when it matters.

name: Teamplay
subtitle: Crossfires, trading, spontaneous organization
description: Work as a unit: crossfires, trading the entry, spontaneous reorganization after a bad split, and the work ethic that keeps a team coherent.

name: Attitude
subtitle: Individual energy, team-grade behavior
description: Promote positive energy, refrain from spilling frustration, and be the teammate that lifts a losing half instead of sinking it.

name: Mental Fortitude
subtitle: Focus when easy, 100% in losses, reset between rounds
description: Stay focused when it's going well, keep playing at 100% when losing, and reset your head between rounds and maps — the hardest habits to train.

name: Decision Making
subtitle: Powerplay, clutches, positioning, reading opponents
description: The moments the round actually swings on: when to powerplay, how to clutch, positioning to read the map, and reading opponents off tempo and economy.

name: CT Side — Team Protocol
subtitle: 5v5 playbook: XvY reads, overotate, timeline, gameplan
description: Team-grade CT protocol: one plan every round, XvY reactions (5v4, 4v5, 3v5), overotate triggers, economy cycle, stuff allocation, timeline 1:55 → setup BP, and gameplan reads against slow / pressure / stack styles.

name: T Side — Team Protocol
subtitle: Classique, zones neutres, finitions BP, afterplant
description: Team-grade T protocol: default classiques (3-1-1, 2-2-1), neutral zones, BP finishers (Limace / Explo / CIS), smoke reserves for late round, afterplant triangles, anti-eco rigor and reading CT overotate to fake.

name: Economy & Stuff Management
subtitle: Save / half / rebuy / forcebuy, molo-smoke-HE-flash split
description: Buy decisions by weapons saved, forcebuy timing (one per map), AWP economy, exit kills, and stuff distribution: 3 molo early / 2 reac, 3 smoke early / 2 reac, 3 HE / 2 double-flash.

name: Team Callouts Protocol
subtitle: Numbers, name, details, deathcam, intensity
description: Call structure: number of players, prénom, info details, deathcam, intensity. Freezetime drop requests with prénom. Retake comms: wait, kit check, priority utility, 40s window.

name: Mentality & Opponent Analysis
subtitle: Trade mindset, stay loose, read their roles
description: Solidarity under pressure, jokes as focus release, trade-mentality as team habit. Opponent reading by pseudo/role (extrémité A, pivot, snipe) and style tag (CIS / STACK / PRESSION). When blocked on a call — change zone, don't grind it.

name: IEM Rio 2026 — Decision Pack
subtitle: Key decision moments from IEM Rio 2026 grand final
description: Tournament-grade pack built from real IEM Rio 2026 grand final rounds. Study the exact fork moments where tier-1 IGLs chose to overotate, fake, or reset — and what you would have done.

name: PGL Major 2025 — Powerplay Pack
subtitle: Anti-eco decisions from the 2025 PGL Major group stage
description: Premium pack focused on powerplay scenarios from the PGL Major 2025. How tier-1 teams denied eco pushes, set punition angles and managed the numerical advantage.

name: Advanced Game Vision
subtitle: Advanced game vision concepts, weak/strong side and map control.
description: Learn how to cut the map in half, numerically identify the weak side, optimize defensive setups at the extremities, understand the advantage of open vs closed zones, and manage your alert and comfort zones.
